title: Gemini OS - Technical Specification Document
content:
Gemini OS - Technical Specification Document
1. Overview
Gemini OS is a web-based application that simulates a desktop computer environment. Its core innovation is that the user interface (UI) for all its internal "applications" is not pre-built. Instead, it is dynamically generated in real-time by a Large Language Model (LLM)—specifically, the Google Gemini API.

The application functions as an interactive loop:

A user interacts with a UI element (e.g., clicking an icon or a button).
This interaction is captured and translated into a structured data object.
The data object, along with a history of recent interactions, is sent to the Gemini API.
The LLM, guided by a detailed system prompt, generates new HTML content representing the updated UI state.
This HTML is streamed back to the client, rendered, and becomes the new interactive surface for the user.
This architecture allows for highly dynamic, creative, and context-aware application experiences that are defined by natural language instructions rather than hard-coded logic.
